turns.svg:
<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#ffffff'/>
  <!-- base plate -->
  <rect x='76' y='360' width='360' height='20' rx='4' fill='#222' opacity='0.6'/>
  <!-- feet -->
  <rect x='110' y='380' width='60' height='20' rx='4' fill='#333'/>
  <rect x='342' y='380' width='60' height='20' rx='4' fill='#333'/>
  <!-- generator body -->
  <rect x='76' y='140' width='360' height='200' rx='20' fill='#2f3e46' stroke='#192024' stroke-width='6'/>
  <!-- ventilation slats -->
  <g fill='#8aa3b2'>
    <rect x='220' y='170' width='160' height='16' rx='6'/>
    <rect x='220' y='196' width='160' height='16' rx='6'/>
    <rect x='220' y='222' width='160' height='16' rx='6'/>
  </g>
  <!-- alternator rotor (left circular part) -->
  <g transform='translate(150,250)'>
    <circle cx='0' cy='0' r='68' fill='#e6e7e9' stroke='#292b2f' stroke-width='6'/>
    <circle cx='0' cy='0' r='44' fill='#ffefc3' stroke='#caa13a' stroke-width='6'/>
    <!-- coil arcs -->
    <path d='M-32 -6 A32 32 0 0 1 32 -6' fill='none' stroke='#b35f00' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round'/>
    <path d='M-28 12 A28 28 0 0 1 28 12' fill='none' stroke='#b35f00' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round'/>
    <circle cx='0' cy='0' r='8' fill='#292b2f'/>
  </g>
  <!-- electrical connector -->
  <rect x='210' y='238' width='36' height='16' rx='4' fill='#121416' stroke='#000' stroke-width='2'/>
  <!-- output cable -->
  <path d='M246 246 C300 260 340 260 360 230' fill='none' stroke='#111' stroke-width='8' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/>
  <!-- lightning bolt representing power output -->
  <polygon points='372,210 420,180 380,180 410,140 335,200 370,200' fill='#ffd42a' stroke='#c28f00' stroke-width='4' stroke-linejoin='round'/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{"svg":"<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#ffffff'/>
  <!-- base plate -->
  <rect x='76' y='360' width='360' height='20' rx='4' fill='#222' opacity='0.6'/>
  <!-- feet -->
  <rect x='110' y='380' width='60' height='20' rx='4' fill='#333'/>
  <rect x='342' y='380' width='60' height='20' rx='4' fill='#333'/>
  <!-- generator body -->
  <rect x='76' y='140' width='360' height='200' rx='20' fill='#2f3e46' stroke='#192024' stroke-width='6'/>
  <!-- ventilation slats -->
  <g fill='#8aa3b2'>
    <rect x='220' y='170' width='160' height='16' rx='6'/>
    <rect x='220' y='196' width='160' height='16' rx='6'/>
    <rect x='220' y='222' width='160' height='16' rx='6'/>
  </g>
  <!-- alternator rotor (left circular part) -->
  <g transform='translate(150,250)'>
    <circle cx='0' cy='0' r='68' fill='#e6e7e9' stroke='#292b2f' stroke-width='6'/>
    <circle cx='0' cy='0' r='44' fill='#ffefc3' stroke='#caa13a' stroke-width='6'/>
    <!-- coil arcs -->
    <path d='M-32 -6 A32 32 0 0 1 32 -6' fill='none' stroke='#b35f00' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round'/>
    <path d='M-28 12 A28 28 0 0 1 28 12' fill='none' stroke='#b35f00' stroke-width='6' stroke-linecap='round'/>
    <circle cx='0' cy='0' r='8' fill='#292b2f'/>
  </g>
  <!-- electrical connector -->
  <rect x='210' y='238' width='36' height='16' rx='4' fill='#121416' stroke='#000' stroke-width='2'/>
  <!-- output cable -->
  <path d='M246 246 C300 260 340 260 360 230' fill='none' stroke='#111' stroke-width='8' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/>
  <!-- lightning bolt representing power output -->
  <polygon points='372,210 420,180 380,180 410,140 335,200 370,200' fill='#ffd42a' stroke='#c28f00' stroke-width='4' stroke-linejoin='round'/>
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Sketching design elements**

I’m thinking about the prongs, which could be represented as rectangles or maybe something simpler like a lightning bolt with an outlet circle. I also want to make sure to draw base feet as rectangles under the body. 

Next, I'll add the bolt as a polygon and use a specific color for the stroke. For the coil, I might draw arcs that resemble semi-circular lines inside the rotor, ensuring everything looks clean without any text. 

I’ll compose the SVG markup and will need to be careful about how I escape quotes in the JSON string.**Crafting SVG content**

I'm planning to use single quotes for the attributes in my SVG. However, I need to remember that the entire JSON string has to be wrapped in double quotes. So, the return will look something like this: `{"svg":"<svg ...>...</svg>"}`. 

Now, I’ll focus on constructing the actual SVG content, making sure to create paths and elements correctly. It will be important to keep my syntax consistent while ensuring everything works as expected.**Creating SVG shapes**

I'm starting to write the SVG with various shapes. The overall structure begins with a base rectangle and continues with feet, body, vents, and rotor circles. Each element has specified widths, heights, and fill colors, like the base in a dark shade and the body in a deep color with a stroke.

I need to add a lightning bolt polygon, ensuring its points are set correctly for a consistent shape. It’s crucial that everything flows together nicely in the design!**Finalizing JSON structure**

I might consider adjusting a few details. While comments are allowed, I may choose not to add any for clarity. I'll use single quotes inside my JSON string since the main string should be wrapped in double quotes, which is perfectly fine. 

I want to ensure there are no literal newlines that could cause issues, although including newlines is technically acceptable. In the end, I’ll return the JSON with the SVG string, ensuring everything is formatted correctly. Let’s get that final response ready!

I’m thinking about the prongs, which could be represented as rectangles or maybe something simpler like a lightning bolt with an outlet circle. I also want to make sure to draw base feet as rectangles under the body. 

Next, I'll add the bolt as a polygon and use a specific color for the stroke. For the coil, I might draw arcs that resemble semi-circular lines inside the rotor, ensuring everything looks clean without any text.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
